name: Robinson, John
resolved_employer: Capitol Services, Inc. (Austin corporate/registered-agent and lien-services firm) — co-founder and President
evidence: Occupation 'President' + employer 'Capitol Services, Inc.' match John H. Robinson, JD (UT Law 1977), co-founder and President of Capitol Services, Inc., an Austin firm founded 1978 providing corporate, registered-agent, UCC and lien services; he retired when Clearview Capital invested in the company in April 2023, consistent with him still being President at the Sept. 2022 gift date. Zip is blank in the filing and 'John Robinson' is a very common name, so the ID rests on the employer/title match rather than address; the fec_donation_count of 2,190 is a common-name aggregation artifact and cannot be attributed to this individual.

affiliations.snippet: Public professional profile lists John Robinson as 'Co-founder, Capitol Services, Inc.' in Austin, Texas, in the legal services industry; the company's own site states it began operations in Austin in 1978 offering 'a comprehensive range of corporate, registered agent, and lien services.'

name: Kolker, Thomas
resolved_employer: Law Office of Thomas L. Kolker — solo attorney, court-appointed receivership practice
evidence: Thomas L. Kolker, licensed in Texas since 1977, is a solo practitioner whose State Bar / directory listings give his address in Austin 78722 — an exact zip match, and consistent with occupation 'Lawyer' / employer 'Self'. The same address is the law office of Virginia Marie Raymond, who made an identical $400 gift to Anderson on the same date, corroborating a shared household. FEC search returned no federal PAC contributions (consistent with fec_donation_count 0); no Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registration found.

name: Witte, Tracy
resolved_employer: Self-employed (occupation reported as 'Researcher'); civic role as Swede Hill Neighborhood Association president
evidence: A Tracy Witte in zip 78702 is documented in Austin Chronicle coverage as president of the Swede Hill Neighborhood Association and secretary of the Organization of Central East Austin — Swede Hill sits inside 78702, corroborating the filing's zip and Central East Austin location. The reported occupation 'Researcher / Self employed' could not be tied to a specific field or institution (the academic Tracy K. Witte is a psychology professor at Auburn University and is a different person). FEC and Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist searches returned no matches; the reported fec_donation_count of 12 could not be attributed to specific committees from public search.

affiliations.snippet: Austin Chronicle quotes 'Tracy Witte, Swede Hill Neighborhood Association President' urging Council to acknowledge that the Austin Revitalization Authority's 'flawed business plan has [led] to a misplaced priority on managing ARA's debt rather than on revitalizing the area.'

Austin Chronicle coverage of the East 11th/12th Street urban-renewal debate identifies Tracy Witte with the Swede Hill neighborhood and the Organization of Central East Austin: 'We're not interested in creating a medical corridor. We're interested in creating a vibrant, mixed-use corridor.'

name: Raymond, Virginia
resolved_employer: Law Office of Virginia Marie Raymond — solo immigration and civil-rights attorney; also teaches composition and literature at Austin Community College
evidence: Virginia Marie Raymond (JD, PhD; Texas bar since 1985) is a solo immigration and civil-rights practitioner in Austin 78722 — an exact zip match, consistent with occupation 'lawyer' / employer 'self-employed'. Her office address is shared with Thomas L. Kolker, who made an identical $400 gift to Anderson on the same date, corroborating a shared household. FEC PAC search surfaced no ideological-PAC contributions attributable to her (fec_donation_count 88 could not be resolved to specific committees via public search); no Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registration found.

affiliations.snippet: Published bio states that between August 2007 and August 2011 Raymond directed the Texas After Violence Project, 'an independent, non-profit narrative and human rights organization' documenting the experiences of people affected by serious violence, jails and prisons.
Her bio lists the Texas Jail Project among the social-justice organizations she has been involved with; the bio also notes she taught literacy at the Travis County Jail for seven years.
Bio lists ADAPT of Texas, a disability-rights organization, among the groups Raymond has worked with over a career in social-justice work dating to 1975.

Bio lists La Pena, an Austin Latina/o arts organization, among the organizations Raymond has been involved with; it also notes she 'started a legal services office for low-income refugees and immigrants that has served thousands of people since 1987.'

name: Searle , Michael
resolved_employer: Aro Group, LLC (Austin political-consulting/public-affairs firm); publicly documented as founder of Searle Strategies, LLC and former Executive Director of the Austin Civic Fund
evidence: Michael Searle is a documented Austin political consultant and former City Hall staffer: aide/chief of staff to District 8 Council Member Ellen Troxclair, then head of the pro-Prop K PAC Citizens for an Accountable Austin and of the nonprofit Austin Civic Fund that funded it, and a City of Austin Tourism Commission appointee. The filing's 'CEO / Aro Group, LLC' is consistent with a political-consulting LLC ('The Aro Group Limited Liability Company' is registered in Austin, TX), but the direct link between the filing's employer string and this individual is not independently corroborated, and the 78745 zip was not confirmed against a public address — hence medium rather than high. FEC individual-contribution search returned no ideological-PAC giving (pro-Israel, pro-Palestine, gun, oil & gas, or defense); no Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registration found under Searle, Aro Group, or Searle Strategies.

affiliations.org: Citizens for an Accountable Austin (political action committee)
affiliations.snippet: "The pro-Prop K political action committee Citizens for an Accountable Austin is headed by Michael Searle, former aide to District 8 Council Member Ellen Troxclair."

affiliations.role: Executive Director / head of the nonprofit
affiliations.snippet: Searle heads the nonprofit Austin Civic Fund, which distributed approximately $137,000 to the Prop K effort; he told the Chronicle, "We operate in compliance with all laws, and do not disclose private donors to our fund."

affiliations.org: Office of Austin City Council Member Ellen Troxclair (District 8)
affiliations.snippet: "Searle, who was an aide to former Council Member Ellen Troxclair... After he left Troxclair's office, Searle got involved with a political action committee funding Proposition K."
"Michael Searle of the Tourism Commission. All three are on a list under 'removal of members'."

Firm 'about' page describes Michael Searle as building Searle Strategies after "years of managing high-stakes policy and corporate campaigns across Texas and the United States"; services span Corporate & Brand Strategy, Marketing & Public Relations, and Public Affairs & Policy.

name: Hunziker, J Emil
evidence: Identity is strongly corroborated: FEC-derived individual-contribution listings show 'J EMIL HUNZIKER (NOT EMPLOYED), (Zip code: 78751)' making repeated small-dollar ActBlue contributions, exactly matching the filing's name, zip 78751, and 'Not Employed' occupation, and consistent with the high federal donation count (278). His federal giving pattern is small-dollar Democratic. FEC search surfaced no contributions to AIPAC/United Democracy Project, DMFI, J Street PAC, pro-Palestine, NRA or other gun-rights, gun-control, oil & gas, or defense-contractor PACs. No Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registration found. A public LinkedIn headline for a 'J Emil Hunziker' in Austin reads 'Retired at Lockheed Martin', but the match is name-and-city only and indicates no executive, board, ownership, or lobbying role, so it is not recorded as a military_defense affiliation.

name: Gruber, John
resolved_employer: Human Rights Campaign (HRC) — Director of National Campaigns
evidence: John Gruber is publicly identified in 2024-2025 news coverage as Director of National Campaigns at the Human Rights Campaign, the national LGBTQ+ advocacy and lobbying organization headquartered in Washington, DC — matching the filing's occupation 'Campaign Director', employer 'HRC', and Washington DC 20010 address. He is staff-level, not listed among HRC's executive team, senior leadership, or board. FEC individual-contribution search found no contributions to pro-Israel, pro-Palestine, gun-rights, gun-control, oil & gas, or defense-contractor PACs; the small federal donation count (5) is consistent with routine individual giving. Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registry: no Gruber registrant (he is DC-based).

affiliations.snippet: Described in the article as the "director of national campaigns at the Human Rights Campaign (HRC)" and "an Ohio native," discussing HRC's electoral mobilization of LGBTQ+ voters.

name: Houston, Ora
resolved_employer: Retired — former Austin City Council Member, District 1 (2015-2019); previously 27 years with the Texas Department of Mental Health and Mental Retardation, and staff for Texas Sen. Gonzalo Barrientos (1999-2003)
evidence: Ora Houston is the first person to hold the Austin City Council District 1 seat (2015-2019), a lifelong resident of the Blackland neighborhood in East Austin (zip 78722). Occupation 'Retired' and the 78722 zip match her documented residence; her $400 gift to Steven Brown is in the District 1 seat she formerly held. No FEC ideological-PAC contributions and no Texas Ethics Commission lobbyist registration surfaced.

affiliations.snippet: Ora Houston was a member of the Austin City Council representing District 1 from 2015 to 2019; elected December 16, 2014 and sworn in January 6, 2015.
Founding member and leader of the group that 'drove geographic representation into Austin city government via a citizens petition and initiative' (the 10-1 district system).
Community resume lists current involvement including life membership in the NAACP.
Community resume lists service as Board Member and Board Secretary of Family ElderCare, and volunteer representative to the board at SafePlace Center for Domestic Violence.

affiliations.org: Imagine Austin Comprehensive Plan Citizens Advisory Task Force; Travis County Model Court for Children and Families; Upper Boggy Creek Neighborhood Planning Team
affiliations.role: Task force member; collaborative council member; vice-chair
affiliations.snippet: Board memberships and commissions listed on her bio page: Citizens Advisory Task Force of the Imagine Austin Comprehensive Plan; collaborative council of the Travis County Model Court for Children and Families; vice-chair, Upper Boggy Creek Neighborhood Planning Team.
